message:
perf(search): eliminate thread-local scratch buffers with zero-alloc byte-level SIMD dot products

Remove all per-thread Vec<f16>/Vec<f32> scratch buffer allocations from
the vector search hot path by operating directly on memory-mapped bytes.

- Add dot_product_f16_bytes_f32() and dot_product_f32_bytes_f32() in simd.rs
  that decode f16/f32 values on the fly using f32x8 SIMD with scalar tails
- Remove F16_SCRATCH/F32_SCRATCH thread-local RefCell<Vec<_>> buffers and
  all four take/return helper functions from search.rs
- Update all four search hot-loops (unfiltered/filtered x F16/F32) to call
  the new byte-level dot product functions directly on mapped memory slices
- Fix config.clone() in two_tier.rs test to prevent move-after-use
- Minor rustfmt reformatting in two_tier.rs test chains

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
